meta.purpose: GraphRAG seed — maps OAS standards to the 8-Track Constitution for curriculum routing in adeline-brain
meta.maintainer: adeline-core/src/types.ts → Track enum is the authority

mappings.subject: English Language Arts
mappings.standard_text: Locate and use text features (e.g., title, headings, captions, bold print, glossaries, indexes, table of contents) to understand informational texts.
mappings.rationale: Builds foundational literacy skills — navigating informational structure is the gateway to all truth-seeking in Adeline 2.0.
mappings.adeline_lesson_hook: How do titles and headings help us know what a book is really about?
mappings.homestead_adaptation: Read seed packet labels, canning jar lids, and farm almanac headings as real informational texts.

mappings.subject: Science
mappings.standard_text: Observe and describe how living things (plants and animals, including humans) depend on the natural world to meet their basic needs of food, water, shelter, and air.
mappings.rationale: Dependency and design in creation — Grade 1 entry point for understanding that life systems reflect intentional order.
mappings.adeline_lesson_hook: Why do all living things need the same basic things? Who designed that system?
mappings.homestead_adaptation: Observe chickens, garden beds, and rainwater collection as living examples of creation's interdependence on your homestead.

neo4j_relationships.reason: Practical stewardship of the created world

mappings.subject: Social Studies
mappings.standard_text: Identify and explain the purpose of rules and laws in the home, school, and community, and describe how authority figures enforce them.
mappings.rationale: First introduction to delegated authority — connects Biblical concept of governing structures (Romans 13) to everyday civic life.
mappings.adeline_lesson_hook: Who made the rules in your house? Why do we need them?
mappings.homestead_adaptation: Discuss property boundaries, water rights, and family 'household laws' as real governance at the homestead scale.

neo4j_relationships.reason: Authority and submission rooted in Scripture

mappings.subject: Health
mappings.standard_text: Identify healthy behaviors that affect personal health, including physical activity, nutrition, sleep, and hygiene.
mappings.rationale: The body as a created system — Grade 1 foundation for treating health as stewardship of God's design, not just medical compliance.
mappings.adeline_lesson_hook: God made your body to work best when you feed it good food and rest. What does your body need today?
mappings.homestead_adaptation: Connect nutrition to growing your own food — garden-to-table meals as a health practice, not just a lifestyle choice.

mappings.subject: English Language Arts
mappings.standard_text: Identify the difference between information provided by pictures and information provided by words in an informational text.
mappings.rationale: Pre-critical-thinking entry point — teaching Grade 1 students that pictures and words can tell different stories is the earliest Witness Protocol skill.
mappings.adeline_lesson_hook: Look at this old photograph. What does it show? Now read the caption. Do they match? Which one do you trust more, and why?
mappings.homestead_adaptation: Compare seed catalog pictures to actual harvest — do the pictures always tell the whole truth?
mappings.witness_protocol_note: Any PRIMARY_SOURCE block generated from this standard must include at least one evidence entry with similarityScore >= 0.85 before rendering.

mappings.subject: English Language Arts
mappings.standard_text: Analyze how the author acknowledges and responds to conflicting evidence or viewpoints by evaluating whether the reasoning is sound and the evidence is relevant and sufficient.
mappings.rationale: Direct Witness Protocol equivalent in ELA — evaluating evidence sufficiency at Grade 8 is the academic form of the 0.85 threshold.
mappings.adeline_lesson_hook: An author has one version of an event. A primary source has another. How do you decide which account bears witness to what actually happened?
mappings.homestead_adaptation: Compare a government agricultural report to a homesteader's journal from the same era — whose account has more to lose by lying?
mappings.witness_protocol_note: Historian agent MUST enforce TRUTH_THRESHOLD = 0.85 on all PRIMARY_SOURCE blocks at this standard. ARCHIVE_SILENT triggers a RESEARCH_MISSION.

neo4j_relationships.reason: Evaluating conflicting narratives is prerequisite to just action

mappings.subject: Science
mappings.standard_text: Examine and interpret data on natural hazards, human impacts on Earth's systems, and evaluate the costs and benefits of resource management strategies.
mappings.rationale: Stewardship science at Grade 8 — land and resource management as a moral and practical responsibility, not merely environmental policy.
mappings.adeline_lesson_hook: What does faithful stewardship of land look like when erosion, drought, or pollution threaten it?
mappings.homestead_adaptation: Design a water conservation plan for your homestead based on local watershed data — real stewardship, not theoretical.

neo4j_relationships.reason: Creation care is the theological root of stewardship science
Resource management involves policy, economics, and property rights

mappings.subject: Social Studies
mappings.standard_text: Analyze the role of citizens in identifying injustice, organizing collective action, and advocating for change within constitutional limits.
mappings.rationale: Biblical justice at Grade 8 — distinguishing righteous advocacy (Micah 6:8) from revolution, within covenant structures of civil order.
mappings.adeline_lesson_hook: What makes a protest righteous? What crosses the line? How did abolitionists, suffragists, and civil rights leaders navigate this?
mappings.homestead_adaptation: Research local zoning or water rights disputes — how did ordinary families advocate for their land within the law?
mappings.witness_protocol_note: Historical claims about specific movements require PRIMARY_SOURCE evidence. ARCHIVE_SILENT must fire if no verified source >= 0.85.

neo4j_relationships.reason: Prophetic justice is rooted in Biblical discipleship, not ideology

mappings.subject: Social Studies
mappings.standard_text: Explain how scarcity, incentives, and trade-offs influence individual and collective economic decisions, including the role of government in markets.
mappings.rationale: Economic decision-making at Grade 8 — connecting scarcity and trade-off theory to Biblical stewardship and honest weights (Proverbs 11:1).
mappings.adeline_lesson_hook: If your family has $200 to spend this month, how do you decide between seeds, livestock feed, and medical supplies? That's economics.
mappings.homestead_adaptation: Build a real household budget for a homestead family — track trade-offs between self-sufficiency investments and immediate needs.

neo4j_relationships.reason: Faithfulness with money is a discipleship issue (Luke 16:10)

mappings.subject: Health
mappings.standard_text: Evaluate the influence of media, technology, and cultural factors on food choices, physical activity, and body image, and develop strategies for making informed health decisions.
mappings.rationale: Critical health literacy at Grade 8 — discerning commercial influence from genuine nutritional wisdom, connecting to the body as God's temple (1 Cor. 6:19).
mappings.adeline_lesson_hook: Who profits when you believe you need that supplement, that diet, or that drug? How do you know when health advice is true?
mappings.homestead_adaptation: Compare a processed food label to a homegrown equivalent — trace the ingredients back to their source and evaluate what you actually know.
mappings.witness_protocol_note: Health claims referencing clinical studies require PRIMARY_SOURCE evidence with witnessCitation including archiveName. No unsourced health claims.

neo4j_relationships.reason: Media influence on health is a historical pattern requiring source evaluation
Caring for the body is an act of stewardship and worship

graphrag_loading_instructions.seed_order: Load Grade 1 nodes first (they are PREREQUISITE_FOR Grade 8 nodes via FEEDS_INTO relationships)
